samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel to serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Assigns the most significant bit of the parallel input to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1. Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.</implementation>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[1:0]" init_value="0">Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.</reg>
        <reg id="valid" init_value="0">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</reg>
        <reg id="data" width_description="[3:0]" init_value="0">Data register to store parallel input.</reg>
        <assign id="dout" value="data[3]"/>
        <assign id="valid_out" value="valid"/>
        
        <always_comb>
            if(cnt == 2'd3) begin
                data <= d;
                cnt <= 2'd0;
                valid <= 1'b1;
            end else begin
                cnt <= cnt + 2'd1;
                valid <= 1'b0;
                data <= {data[2:0], data[3]};
            end
        end
        
    </logic_description>
    
</module>
